summary: The React UI is real, buildable, and meaningfully integrated with the backend, and it fulfills the dashboard/visualization promise better than the retained Streamlit tooling. The main risks are reviewer trust and polish: lint fails, there is no frontend test coverage, and demo/generated fallback behavior can weaken evidentiary clarity.

checks.item: Public React UI clearly distinguished from internal Streamlit tooling

evidence: No test/spec files found under frontend/greennet-ui
No frontend test runner dependency found

major_risks: Generated demo runs can be substituted when backend run listing fails, which risks reviewer confusion.
Generated topology/layout fallback reduces evidentiary clarity for dashboard and simulator views.
Lint failure weakens the claim that the frontend is polished and final-submission ready.
No frontend tests or smoke checks exist for a UI that presents final evaluation artifacts.

critical_before_submission: Fix frontend lint errors and warnings.
Make demo-mode state impossible to miss on dashboard and simulator routes.
Clarify in the simulator UI which elements are simulated presentation versus backend-derived state.
important_not_blocking: Add at least one frontend smoke test for the results page.
Add a reviewer note recommending the order Home -> Results -> Dashboard -> Simulator.
Keep Streamlit clearly internal in all final docs and demos.
nice_to_have: Add provenance badges such as artifact-backed, live-run, generated-demo, generated-layout.
Split the large frontend bundle for better engineering polish.
